content.full_text:
Roy W. Lockwood
329 Lo-C-At
Oxnard, CA 93030
July 20, 1990
(805) 483-3134

Campaign Mgmt
San Luis Obispo Commission
P.O. Box 94401
Sacramento CA 94244-4001.

I'm a Candidate for the Oxnard Unified
High school District Board of Trustee.

I intend to file a Compaign statement only! Estimated Cost 19 950.00.

Do it necessary that I open an
Bank Account & File forms 501 & 502
just for $9,500.0.

I plan no further expence than
the $250.00 filing fee(Returned).

Could one be paid by Casher Check?
Since at's under the $1,000.00 would
the 501 & 502 forms be waived?

Thank you for your assistance
respectfully,

Roy W Lockwood

sections.question: Is it necessary that I open a Bank Account & File Forms 501 & 502 just for $750.00? Could this be paid by Cashier Checks? Since it's under the $1,000.00 would the 501 & 502 process be waived?
sections.question_synthetic: A candidate for school board is asking whether they must open a bank account and file Forms 501 and 502 for a campaign with only $750 in estimated expenses, and whether such filing requirements can be waived for amounts under $1,000.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No formal question/conclusion sections are present. The document contains a candidate's inquiries about campaign finance filing thresholds and procedures. The second part of the document appears to be unrelated scanning artifact (faint mathematical content) and was disregarded.

embedding.summary: Roy W. Lockwood, a candidate for Oxnard Union High School District Board of Trustees, submitted an incoming request to the FPPC asking whether campaign finance filing requirements (bank account opening and Forms 501 & 502) apply to a minimal campaign with only $750 in expenses, and whether cashier checks could be used for payment.
